scenarios.opening_query: My record from my twenties keeps costing me apartments and I'm 44 years old now.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Expunction || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The record was already sealed two years ago — the screening companies keep reporting it anyway, and suing the background-check vendors for reporting sealed records is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was sealed two years ago — the screening companies report it anyway. Suing those vendors is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether vendors are reporting an already-sealed record
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Never sealed — the set-aside petition itself is the missing step.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was never sealed — the petition is the missing step.

scenarios.opening_query: My fiancé has an old warrant in another state and we can't start our life with this hanging over us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'warrant' is actually a probation-violation judgment entered in absentia years ago — vacating that in-absentia judgment in the other state is appellate/post-judgment work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's an in-absentia probation-violation judgment from years ago — vacating that is post-judgment work in the other state.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an in-absentia judgment must be vacated
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a plain bench warrant on an unresolved misdemeanor — local counsel there can arrange a walk-through arraignment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A plain bench warrant on an open misdemeanor — a walk-through arraignment handles it.

scenarios.opening_query: The police still have my phone from a case that ended months ago and 'evidence' won't return calls.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The case ended in full dismissal and no forfeiture was ever filed — after four written demands, the retention is just unlawful, and a replevin/civil-rights demand letter from counsel typically shakes it loose.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Full dismissal, no forfeiture ever filed, four written demands ignored — the retention is unlawful and a counsel demand shakes it loose.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether any legal basis for retention exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: A civil forfeiture petition over the phone was filed last month — contesting it inside that case is the route.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They filed a forfeiture petition last month — contest it in that case.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's college expelled her over an incident the police themselves decided wasn't worth charging.
scenarios.hidden_fact: With no charges anywhere, the fight is the university's conduct process — no hearing, no evidence shared, a decision by email; the due-process appeal is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: No charges exist — the fight is the university's process: no hearing, no evidence, decision by email.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether only the school proceeding remains
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DA reversed course last week and filed — the criminal case now leads and the school case follows it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The DA reversed and filed last week — the criminal case leads now.

scenarios.opening_query: I did everything diversion required and the court still shows my case as open a year later.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The dismissal order was entered — the clerk just never transmitted it to the state repository; a record-correction and sealing petition finishes what diversion earned.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The dismissal was entered — the clerk never transmitted it. Record correction and sealing finish what diversion earned.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the case is legally closed with a records failure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's genuinely open — a compliance box was never checked and the DA can still reinstate; closing it properly is defense work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's genuinely open — a compliance box was missed and reinstatement is possible.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother called 911 during his crisis and somehow ended up the one facing consequences.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Mental Commitment
scenarios.hidden_fact: No charges were filed — the 'consequences' are a civil commitment petition from the crisis team, with a hearing in six days where he needs counsel.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: No charges — it's a civil commitment petition from the crisis team, hearing in six days.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the proceeding is commitment rather than criminal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They charged misdemeanor menacing from things said during the crisis — a criminal defense with a mental-health context.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They charged misdemeanor menacing from the crisis — criminal defense with mental-health context.
